issue_title: We need an event based risk calculator starting from the GMFs
issue_body:
In the past most (virtually all) risk calculation running in a cluster where dominated by the risk part, thus it made sense to recompute the GMFs on-the-fly every time. That had the advantage of reducing the data transfer significantly. For instance for Colombia the GMFs are 100 GB, the ruptures, 0.5 GB, so the data transfer is reduced by 200 times by recomputing the GMFs on the fly.

However things are different now. We introduced recently the `minimum_intensity` feature and that changes the rules of the game. It is now possible to filter away the GMFs below the minimum intensity, and that means that over 99% of the GMFs can be removed (in the case of Colombia with minimum_intensity PGA=0.1 the 100 GB becomes 0.8 GB). This kind of reduction is typical for our calculations. So it makes sense to compute the GMFs once and to store them since after the filtering they are few and easy to transfer.

Also, now the GMFs are stored in the HDF5 format in an ultra-efficient way (and they will be stored in an even more efficient way in the future, using a new feature of h5py ) so it is not an issue to store even terabytes of them (a storing speed of 500+ MB/s has been measured on Wilson). Also transferring
hundreds of the gigabytes is achievable if the transfer is done carefully (i.e. don't try to transfer everything at once).

Due to those advances, saving the GMFs has become reasonable now. Not only that, having the GMFs on disk allow different parallelization strategies for the risk that will result in a big speedup of the risk part. Moreover computing the GMFs in the risk part has a big memory cost, whereas computing them in the hazard has a much smaller memory cost due to the differences in the algorithm used.

NB: the current calculator based on the rupture has to be maintained because there are still computations dominated by the risk part and where it makes sense to recompute the GMFs on the fly.
The user will be able to select the old calculator or the new one by specifying the calculation_mode in the job.ini file. `event_based_risk` or `ebr_from_gmf`.


PS: having a calculator starting from the GMFs is needed for two other reasons, apart from performance:

1. it makes it easier to debug the calculators
2. third party can give us the GMFs and we can start the risk from there.

issue_title: The version number is wrong on Jenkins
issue_body:
See for instance https://ci.openquake.org/job/master_oq-engine/2503/consoleFull, there is an output

```
[2016-06-13 22:24:54,428 #1 INFO] Using engine version 2.0.0
```

whereas it should be something like
```
[2016-06-13 22:24:54,428 #1 INFO] Using engine version 2.0.0-gitaff1043
```

HINT: look at the sed mangling in the packager, lines
```
# version info from openquake/risklib/__init__.py
ini_vers="$(cat openquake/risklib/__init__.py | sed -n "s/^__version__[  ]*=[    ]*['\"]\([^'\"]\+\)['\"].*/\1/gp")"
ini_maj="$(echo "$ini_vers" | sed -n 's/^\([0-9]\+\).*/\1/gp')"
ini_min="$(echo "$ini_vers" | sed -n 's/^[0-9]\+\.\([0-9]\+\).*/\1/gp')"
ini_bfx="$(echo "$ini_vers" | sed -n 's/^[0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+\.\([0-9]\+\).*/\1/gp')"
ini_suf="$(echo "$ini_vers" | sed -n 's/^[0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+\(.*\)/\1/gp')"
```

    from vendored import boto3   # Importing from a folder that I used "pip install -t vendored/ boto3" on

issue_title: Applying sigv4 to requests to URLs wtih a path containing '.' or '..' can fail
issue_body:
This happens if `.` or `..` are at the beginning of a path segment: they get stripped out, but that's correct only if the whole path segment is `.` or `..`.

For example, generating a signature for a request to `https://service.amazonaws.com/path/.somepath/` would be rejected by the services, because when the path is normalised, it gets converted to `/path/somepath/`.

See pull request: #827

issue_body:

issue_title: Introduce a mechanism to revoke JWTs
issue_body:
We will need a default way to revoke tokens in order to:

  * Deny access
  * Change implementation
  * Force user to re-login in order to update token data (e.g. added/removed a role)

If a user calls `DriverStation.getStickButton` in `robotInit()` (for example, when initializing a `JoystickButton` command) , they'll get a warning that the button does not exist yet. This happens because the initial count of all buttons is zero, and the count doesn't get updated until the DS thread executes at least a single loop.

WPILib seems like it would have the same issue, except that presumably their thread gets executed in time and so the warning never occurs (or people just ignore it).

A workaround could be just to initialize the button count to the max number. Once the ds thread updates, once the user calls the thing again they'll get the warning if it's needed.

Reference: https://www.chiefdelphi.com/forums/showthread.php?threadid=166029

issue_title: Should I commit pipfile.lock to my Python package repo?
issue_body:
Note: `https://kenneth-reitz.uservoice.com/forums/913660-general` says "This site is not currently active" and I think this query is too speculative for StackOverflow, so I've posted the query here. (If there's an official answer, then it stops being speculative ;D )

From the (excellent) documentation, the semantics of a pipenv's pipfile is a loosely-versioned list of the dependencies for the package. The pipfile.lock is an environment-specific list of the exact dependencies installed.

So, my hunch is that I *must* commit my pipfile to my package repo (otherwise how will you know what the dependencies are?) but I *must not* commit my pipfile.lock (what if I use GNU/Linux and you use Windows). Is this correct?

issue_title: Provide an environment variable pointing to the project root
issue_body:
##### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I'm working on a few projects that are configured using environment variables, thus I was quite happy that the `.env` file for setting them exists. While this works great for most environment variables, it becomes cumbersome as soon as I need to specify a path relative to the project root. My naive approach was:

`VARIABLE=${PWD}/some_directory`

As long as `pipenv run/shell` is run from the project root directory, this works great. But if `pipenv run/shell` is run from a subdirectory of the project root, this does not work any longer, because `PWD` points to the wrong directory in this case. By the way, this also spoils the joy of using pipes to go to your project directory, because the `PWD` variable still points to the current directory (e.g., `~`) when the `.env` file is evaluated.

##### Describe the solution you'd like

I'd like to have an environment variable pointing to the project directory set before the `.env` file is parsed, e.g., something like `PIPENV_PROJECT`, so the following assignment in `.env` would work as expected irrespective of the current working directory:

`VARIABLE=${PIPENV_PROJECT}/some_directory`

It would certainly do no harm if this variable would be available not only for parsing the `.env` file, but also for the shell or for the command started using pipenv as well.

##### Describe alternatives you've considered

The obvious alternative I found is putting absolute paths into the `.env` file. But then I can't add that file to version control, which is the main reason for me wanting to use it in the first place.

##### Additional context

I'm using pipenv 2018.7.1 installed via pip on a CentOS 7 machine.

issue_title: extension with py_limited_api=True not linked with PYTHON3.DLL
issue_body:
I tried to write a simple CPython extension module that just prints "hello world" in order to try out the `py_limited_api=True` option of setuptools `Extension` class.
The code can be found here: https://github.com/anthrotype/hello-world-cpython-extension

When I build on macOS or Linux, the generated extension module has `abi3` suffix and can be loaded on different CPython versions (e.g. 3.5 and 3.6).

On Windows however, the module (which doesn't get `abi3` suffix, but is simply called `hello_world.pyd` when the `py_limited_api=True` option is set) cannot be imported by different CPython 3.x minor versions.

Dependency Walker says that the .pyd file is linked with PYTHON36.DLL, whereas I would have expected it to be linked with the stable API version of that which is called PYTHON3.DLL.

I came across this issue while debugging another related issue with `bdist_wheel --py-limited-api` that raises `AssertionError` on Windows because `abi3` tag isn't supported on that platform:
https://github.com/hynek/argon2_cffi/pull/32

cc @agronholm @dholt @zooba @pfmoore @dstufft @jaraco etc.

Trying to update the package in Fedora from 36.2.0 to 36.5.0 produces some test failures with the message:
`RuntimeError: maximum recursion depth exceeded while calling a Python object`

Attaching two different build logs where tests are invoked under python2 and under python3 respectively.

[py2build.log](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/files/1376480/py2build.log)


[py3build.log](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/files/1376473/py3build.log)

issue_title: entry_points specified in setup.cfg may fail silently
issue_body:
I recently moved my setup.py contents to setup.cfg using the metadata/options sections.
It works well with the latest setuptools, but I found a small glitch:

* When `entry_points` are specified in **setup.py**, running `pip install -e .` reports "invalid group name" if the entry point group name has invalid characters. 
* When the same `entry_points` are specified in **setup.cfg**, it goes silenty without reporting any warning or errors and the entry point does not work.

I think the error-reporting behavior for both cases should be consistent.

issue_body: Caused by pypa/pip#2395 but just wanted to make sure this is tracked/fixed in virtualenv as well since pip is bundled.

issue_title: Install a profile from the command line
issue_body:
I might replace some custom scripts by using ftw.upgrade from the command line. One thing that I then miss is the ability to install a profile. Would that be something you would consider including in your tool or would it be out of scope?
It has got nothing to do with upgrade steps, so that might make it too much. Perhaps I should do this with collective.recipe.plonesite, which supports this already. But for me it feels close to what ftw.upgrade already does.
I guess the alternative is that if I want to install new package B I can write an upgrade step for an already installed package A that does this. But that feels a bit 'meh' to me.

For reference, here is my current script, which I can of course still keep even if I replace a few others:

```
$ cat scripts/install_products.py 
# Run this with:
# bin/instance run scripts/install_products.py
from Products.CMFCore.utils import getToolByName
from zope.component.hooks import setSite
import transaction

products = [
    # 'collective.flowplayer',
    'CMFPlacefulWorkflow',
    'plone.app.dexterity',
    'clientname.jsonapi',
    ]

print('')
print('Will install these products:')
for product in products:
    print('- %s' % product)

# Get all Plone Sites.
# app is the Zope root.
plones = [obj for obj in app.objectValues()
          if getattr(obj, 'portal_type', '') == 'Plone Site']

for site in plones:
    print('')
    print('Handling Plone Site %s.' % site.id)
    setSite(site)
    qi = getToolByName(site, 'portal_quickinstaller')
    # Keep list of products that we are installing.
    installed = []
    for product in products:
        if qi.isProductInstalled(product):
            print('Already installed: %s' % product)
            continue
        if not qi.isProductInstallable(product):
            print('WARNING: not installable: %s' % product)
            continue
        print('Installing: %s' % product)
        qi.installProduct(product)
        installed.append(product)
        print('Installed: %s' % product)
    if installed:
        print('Committing transaction for Plone Site %s.' % site.id)
        # Add transaction note.
        tr = transaction.get()
        tr.note('Installed products in %s: %s' % (
            site.id, ', '.join(installed)))
        transaction.commit()
print('Done.')
```

issue_title: condenseFlow in combination with flowLevel 0 is broken
issue_body:
Simple sample code:

```
var jsYaml = require("js-yaml")
console.log(jsYaml.safeDump({ a: "a", b: "b", c: "c" }, { flowLevel: 0, condenseFlow: true }))
```

This results in a broken string like this:
```
{"a":a", b":b", c":c}\n
```

Without condense flow the result works as expected:
```
{a: a, b: b, c: c}\n
```

issue_body:

    from django.conf.urls import patterns, url

    from security import views

Should be:

    import django
    from django.conf.urls import url

    from security import views

issue_title: Efficiently pulling latest message from a topic
issue_body:
I am interested in a method to efficiently pull the last message from an existing consumer

After a little bit of digging I've come up with the following incantation

```python
consumer = client = KafkaClient(hosts="xxxxxxx")
topic = client.topics['mytopic']
consumer = topic.get_simple_consumer()

def consume_latest():
     latest_offsets = [
           (partition, pykafka.common.OffsetType.LATEST) for partition in consumer._partitions.keys()
        ]
     consumer.reset_offsets(latest_offsets)
     # now step the offset back by one to fetch the latest...
     almost_latest_offsets = []
     for partition, owned_partition in consumer._partition.items():
         almost_latest_offsets.append(
              (partition, owned_partition.next_offset - 1)
          )

     consumer.reset_offsets(almost_latest_offsets)
     return consumer.consume()
```

which looks a little funky but to me but I _think_ it should work. However because of the funkiness I wanted to check if there might be better way to do it?

issue_title: Change psf_photometry function to class-based architecture
issue_body:
This came up in one of the GSoC 2016 hangouts, where I think we came to agreement, but I'm leaving it here as a reminder to actually implement this.

The basic idea is to change the high-level PSF routines to be callable classes, a la the work in #379 and #370 .  So the idea would be to replace the current `psf_photometry` function with a callable class that contains the configurable bits (for `psf_photometry` that's essentially just the model and the fitter, I think).

The simplest approach would be to just take that function, move its guts to a `do_photometry` function and build it as a totally separate implementation but with the same interface (and perhaps a shared base class) like `DAOPhotPSFPhotometry` from #385.  But @mirca and @hamogu pointed out that actually what `psf_phometry` does is probably entirely subsumed by `DAOPhotPSFPhotometry`, as `psf_photometry` is basically that but with no iterative DAOPHOT-style subtracting and re-finding. So it _might_ make more sense to instead have the `psf_photometry` replacement class be a subclass of `DAOPhotPSFPhotometry` and just call it with many of the parameters hard-coded.

A few more details that come to mind for me:
- If we go with this plan, I suggest some name changes that make it clear what these classes actually do.  The `psf_photometry()` replacement class could be something like `SimplePSFPhotometry` and `DAOPhotPSFPhotometry` could be changed to something slightly more descriptive of what it actually does like `IterativeSubtractionPSFPhotometry`.
- The current `psf_photometry` function uses a clever trick to support `NDData` objects as inputs: it uses the `astropy.nddata.support_nddata` decorator.  We'll want this class to do something similar so that it can just be called with an `nddata` object, but whether that should use `support_nddata` or instead should be something in the `__call__` or `do_photometry` method is an implementation question.
- The example notebooks in https://github.com/astropy/photutils-datasets will need to be updated to use the new class instead of `psf_photmetry()`

Note that @mirca volunteered to work on this (it won't let me assign him to the issue for reasons I'm trying to fix, but this will suffice as a "soft assign")

cc @mirca @hamogu @bsipocz

issue_title: RectangularAperture creating masks that are too large
issue_body:
I'm trying to use ``photutils.RectangularAperture`` to define apertures in Shack-Hartmann wavefront sensor data.  Rather than do photometry, though, I need to calculate centroids within each aperture.  So I use ``photutils.RectangularAperture.to_mask()`` to  generate the list of masks, loop through that list, and use ``ApertureMask.cutout()`` to generate the sub-image to centroid for each mask.  However, ``to_mask()`` is generating masks that are much larger than the apertures I'm defining.  In my case, ``w = h = 22`` for the apertures, but the masks being generated have a shape of ``(32, 32)``.  This looks suspiciously like a ``sqrt(2)`` scaling bug somewhere.

My workaround for now is to use ``CircularAperture`` which properly generates ``(23, 23)`` sub-images.

issue_title: bbox_area returns the area of the whole input image, not the area of the bounding box of a labeled region
issue_body:
## Description
When using the bbox_area method, the returned value is input_data.size instead of labeled_bounding_box.size.


## Way to reproduce
```python
from skimage import data, util
from skimage.measure import label
from skimage.measure import regionprops

img = util.img_as_ubyte(data.coins()) > 110
label_img = label(img, connectivity=img.ndim)
props = regionprops(label_img, intensity_image=data.coins())

print(props[86].bbox_area)
print(props[86].image.size
print(data.coins().size)
```

issue_title: Appveyor builds take to long to be useful.
issue_body: The Appveyor builds run in serial, as opposed to Travis.  I suggest we limit the Appveyor build to one 32bit build.  The Python 3.4 build has been randomly timing out, so I'd suggest the Python 2.7 one.

issue_title: Add support for multipage tiff files with the freeimage plugin
issue_body:
Currently, our Freeimage TIFF reader just reads the first plane of a multipage tiff and calls it a day. It would be good to figure out how to support multipage tiff properly. (Though tifffile handles it admirably, users might set freeimage for whatever reason, and be stuck with buggy code.)

See #1440 for a related issue.

```
    def merge_nodes(self, src, dst, weight_func=min_weight, in_place=True,
                    extra_arguments=[], extra_keywords={}):
        """Merge node `src` and `dst`.
    
            The new combined node is adjacent to all the neighbors of `src`
            and `dst`. `weight_func` is called to decide the weight of edges
            incident on the new node.
    
            Parameters
            ----------
            src, dst : int
                Nodes to be merged.
            weight_func : callable, optional
                Function to decide the attributes of edges incident on the new
                node. For each neighbor `n` for `src and `dst`, `weight_func` will
                be called as follows: `weight_func(src, dst, n, *extra_arguments,
                **extra_keywords)`. `src`, `dst` and `n` are IDs of vertices in the
                RAG object which is in turn a subclass of `networkx.Graph`. It is
                expected to return a dict of attributes of the resulting edge.
            in_place : bool, optional
                If set to `True`, the merged node has the id `dst`, else merged
                node has a new id which is returned.
            extra_arguments : sequence, optional
                The sequence of extra positional arguments passed to
                `weight_func`.
            extra_keywords : dictionary, optional
                The dict of keyword arguments passed to the `weight_func`.
    
            Returns
            -------
            id : int
                The id of the new node.
    
            Notes
            -----
            If `in_place` is `False` the resulting node has a new id, rather than
            `dst`.
            """
        src_nbrs = set(self.neighbors(src))
        dst_nbrs = set(self.neighbors(dst))
        neighbors = (src_nbrs | dst_nbrs) - set([src, dst])
    
        if in_place:
            new = dst
        else:
            new = self.next_id()
            self.add_node(new)
    
        for neighbor in neighbors:
            data = weight_func(self, src, new, neighbor, *extra_arguments,
                               **extra_keywords)
            self.add_edge(neighbor, new, attr_dict=data)

        """Compute the Region Adjacency Graph using mean colors.
    
        Given an image and its initial segmentation, this method constructs the
        corresponding Region Adjacency Graph (RAG). Each node in the RAG
        represents a set of pixels within `image` with the same label in `labels`.
        The weight between two adjacent regions represents how similar or
        dissimilar two regions are depending on the `mode` parameter.
    
        Parameters
        ----------
        image : ndarray, shape(M, N, [..., P,] 3)
            Input image.
        labels : ndarray, shape(M, N, [..., P])
            The labelled image. This should have one dimension less than
            `image`. If `image` has dimensions `(M, N, 3)` `labels` should have
            dimensions `(M, N)`.
        connectivity : int, optional
            Pixels with a squared distance less than `connectivity` from each other
            are considered adjacent. It can range from 1 to `labels.ndim`. Its
            behavior is the same as `connectivity` parameter in
            ``scipy.ndimage.generate_binary_structure``.
        mode : {'distance', 'similarity'}, optional
            The strategy to assign edge weights.
    
                'distance' : The weight between two adjacent regions is the

                colors of the two regions. It represents the Euclidean distance in
                their average color.
    
                'similarity' : The weight between two adjacent is
                :math:`e^{-d^2/sigma}` where :math:`d=|c_1 - c_2|`, where
                :math:`c_1` and :math:`c_2` are the mean colors of the two regions.
                It represents how similar two regions are.
```

issue_title: threshold_niblack and threshold_sauvola sometimes return NaN when calling them with float64 images
issue_body:
## Description

`threshold_niblack` and `threshold_sauvola` sometimes return NaN when calling them with float64 images

## Way to reproduce
- [X] Code example

```
# the code below will return NaN values only.
# depending on the image's pixel values,
# it's possible to get back a mix of NaN and float values

import numpy as np
from skimage import filters

# 16 pixels, single channel
src_img = np.array([0.03082192 + 2.19178082e-09] * 16).astype('float64')
src_img = src_img.reshape((4,4))

# will print array of 4x4 NaNs
print(filters.threshold_niblack(src_img))

# will print array of 4x4 NaNs
print(filters.threshold_sauvola(src_img))
```

the reason for the NaNs is that `sqrt` is being called in [_mean_std](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/filters/thresholding.py#L775) with negative numbers.

- [X] Operating system and version

OS X 10.11.6

- [X] Python version

2.7.10

- [X] scikit-image version (run `skimage.__version__`)

0.14dev

I am using the last commit as of now, da0800a2a0c4abb51c621ff495c3297cc69d43a1
please push this package to pypi

issue_title: Destroy Segment on its Last Synapse Deletion
issue_body: Currently the synapses are "culled" when their permanences are decremented below a known setting so that the HTM can adapt to a totally new set of inputs and readjust itself. When the last Synapse is destroyed, the Segment should be deleted also in order to increase performance and memory associated with the obsolete Segment. There are loops which are governed by the number of segments on a cell which will benefit from a decrease in their iterative content, so these need to be destroyed along with the last Synapse.

issue_title: Py and C++ classes use same init params
issue_body:
EDIT: This issue is to enforce that py and C++ use the same init params. Doing otherwise is against the principle that those 2 implementations are identical. This is fixing (unfortunately not, but cornering) a real problem where SDR representations differ in SP in Py/C++ implementations. 

- [ ] blocked by: https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/628

In the TP's `inferPhase2` function, there's this comment:

    The computes the predicted state, then
    checks to insure that the predicted state is not over-saturated, i.e.
    look too close like a burst. This indicates that there were so many
    separate paths learned from the current input columns to the predicted
    input columns that bursting on the current input columns is most likely
    generated mix and match errors on cells in the predicted columns. If
    we detect this situation, we instead turn on only the start cells in the
    current active columns and re-generate the predicted state from those.

As far as I can see, there's no such logic going on in the function.

issue_title: Remove nupic/research and move algorithm code to nupic/algorithms
issue_body:
It is very confusing to have both nupic/research and nupic/algorithms, both of which contain algorithms. We currently import many algorithms from nupic.research instead of nupic.algorithms  It is even more confusing given that we have a nupic.research repository which is yet another thing.

This issue is to move algorithm code from nupic/research to nupic/algorithms, and to delete nupic/research altogether. As part of this we would update all tests and resolve any backward incompatibilities.

Some of the code in nupic/research, such as monitor_mixin, fdrutilities, and utils.py don't belong in nupic/algorithms and should probably move to nupic/support or some other place.
Part of #2000.

issue_title: Extending BibDoc restriction to allow mixing firerole rules/roles/groups
issue_body:
Originally on 2012-08-22

Currently BibDocs can be restricted via `FFT`} tags by using the `$r` subfield according to the syntax described in [http://invenio-demo.cern.ch/help/admin/bibupload-admin-guide#3.6].

So far, admins can specify the restriction of a BibDoc by setting either:
* a single *status* matching a WebAccess authorization for the action `viewrestrdoc`
* a single WebAccess *role*
* a single *group*
* a single *email address*
* a single *firerole rule*

It is advisible to be able to combine the above and let the admin to specify e.g. a firerole rule **and** a group or multiple groups, etc.

issue_body:
Originally on 2013-07-15

Currently the BibRank method `index_term_count` defined in `bibrank_tag_based_indexer` does not let the admin to specify the collections from which to consider occurrencies of the term.

For example, currently, in INSPIRE there is a piece of configuration such as:

``` ini
[rank_method]
function = index_term_count

[index_term_count]
index_table_name = idxPHRASE10F
index_term_value_from_tag = 110__u
relevance_number_output_prologue = (
relevance_number_output_epilogue = )
```

to define the `papers in HEP` rank to be used to order [HEP institutions](http://inspirehep.net/collection/Institutions)

It is desirable to be able to specify a (list of) collection(s), from which to pick up matching recids. (otherwise the current computation is also done on hidden collections, restricted ones, not related one, etc.)

issue_title: runzeo problem with opening storages obscured by cleanup code
issue_body:
In:

    def main(self):
        self.setup_default_logging()
        self.check_socket()
        self.clear_socket()
        self.make_pidfile()
        try:
            self.open_storages()
            self.setup_signals()
            self.create_server()
            self.loop_forever()
        finally:
            self.server.close()
            self.clear_socket()
            self.remove_pidfile()

If there's a failure in or before create_server, the first statement in the finally block will fail, hiding the original error.

issue_title: `unregisterAdapter` uses '==' while the underlying adapter registry uses 'is'
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/551049, @mgedmin reported:

> Try this:

```
class MyTestClass(PlacelessSetUp):

    def _handler(self, event):
        print "Got event!"

    def test(self):
        sm = zope.component.getGlobalSiteManager()
        sm.registerHandler(self._handler, required=(zope.interface.Interface, ))
        assert sm.unregisterHandler(self._handler, required=(zope.interface.Interface, ))
        zope.event.notify(object())
```

> The assertion will demonstrate that the global site manager thinks it successfully unregistered the adapter, yet the subsequent notify() will invoke our handler.
> 
> Why? Because self._handler creates a new bound method instance every time you write that expression. sm.unregisterHandler compares it to the previously registered factory object using '==' which returns true. But then the site manager invokes zope.interface.adapter.BaseAdapterRegistry.unsubscribe, which uses 'is' for identity checking and therefore thinks it got a different handler.
> 
> zope.component should use the same kind of check as the underlying implementation, because it now lies to the programmer about unregistering an adapter that really remains registered.

@d03cc followed up:

> Trying to reproduce it, I noticed that the code that does not do the proper unregistration is in zope.interface and not in zope.component

issue_title: Interface comparison with emtpy __name__ reports incorrect equality
issue_body:
When comparing two different interfaces with the same, empty name they are reported to be equal:

zope.interface version 4.1.3:

```
>>> from zope.interface import Interface
>>> class A(Interface): pass
... 
>>> class B(Interface): pass
... 
>>> A == B  # correctly repots unquality
False

>>> A.__name__ = ''
>>> B.__name__ = ''
>>> A == B  # equality looks incorrect to me.
True
```

With zope.interface 3.6.7 the last test returns `False`, so they are treated as unequal.

Also, the `zope.interface.interface.InterfaceClass.__cmp` function comment says ( https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.interface/blob/master/src/zope/interface/interface.py#L490 )

```
        # Yes, I did mean to name this __cmp, rather than __cmp__.
        # It is a private method used by __lt__ and __gt__.
        # I don't want to override __eq__ because I want the default
        # __eq__, which is really fast.
```

But the `__eq__` function was added with https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.interface/commit/c7a10ce52ad99fb6528416678fc057e5338d3e2b -- the comment should be changed.
Related: https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/570942

issue_title: When installed inside Tox: UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xc2 in position 388: ordinal not in range(128)
issue_body:
Some Home Assistant developers have reported an issue installing RestrictedPython 4.0a2 inside tox because of a UnicodeDecode error when `setup.py` reads the README file. Our issue is here: https://github.com/home-assistant/home-assistant/issues/8026

Installing outside Tox does not give us any problems.

```
Collecting restrictedpython==4.0a2 (from -r /home/alex/home-assistant/requirements_test_all.txt (line 107))
  Using cached RestrictedPython-4.0a2.tar.gz
    Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
      File "/tmp/pip-build-faptnbow/restrictedpython/setup.py", line 33, in <module>
        long_description=(read('README.rst') + '\n' +
      File "/tmp/pip-build-faptnbow/restrictedpython/setup.py", line 23, in read
        return open(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), *rnames)).read()
      File "/home/alex/home-assistant/.tox/py36/lib/python3.6/encodings/ascii.py", line 26, in decode
        return codecs.ascii_decode(input, self.errors)[0]
    UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xc2 in position 388: ordinal not in range(128)
 ```

We set our language inside tox to UTF-8 ([source](https://github.com/home-assistant/home-assistant/blob/dev/tox.ini#L8-L13)):

```
install_command = /usr/bin/env LANG=C.UTF-8 pip install {opts} {packages}
```

I guess a potential solution could be to pass in `encoding='utf8'` to the open call.

According to TravisCI there are no test failures on Python 3.7: https://travis-ci.org/zopefoundation/RestrictedPython/builds/333655708

To officially support this version the Python 3.7 change log has to be evaluated if there are changes which have to be reflected in RestrictedPython.

issue_title: Django 1.10: AttributeError: 'Foo' object has no attribute '_deferred'
issue_body:
> The Model._deferred attribute is removed as dynamic model classes when using QuerySet.defer() and only() is removed.

https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.10/releases/1.10/

The fix is:

```diff
diff --git a/fsm_admin/templatetags/fsm_admin.py b/fsm_admin/templatetags/fsm_admin.py
index 3b7e1fb..086c989 100644
--- a/fsm_admin/templatetags/fsm_admin.py
+++ b/fsm_admin/templatetags/fsm_admin.py
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@ import logging
 from django import template
 from django.contrib.admin.templatetags.admin_modify import submit_row
 from django.conf import settings
+from django.db import models
 
 register = template.Library()
 
@@ -47,7 +48,7 @@ def fsm_submit_row(context):
     original = context.get('original', None)
     model_name = ''
     if original is not None:
-        if original._deferred:
+        if original is models.DEFERRED:
             model_name = type(original).__base__._meta.verbose_name
         else:
             model_name = original.__class__._meta.verbose_name

```

issue_title: Handle jwt Issued by Another Service
issue_body:
Right now I have another service issuing `jwts` and I want to use these tokens for authentication on a DRF service. I am able to get `django-rest-framework-jwt` to validate the token and extract the `user_id` but then I get down to [here](https://github.com/GetBlimp/django-rest-framework-jwt/blob/6b763b58996676c8362e54caef1f4ff62e5c676a/rest_framework_jwt/authentication.py#L70) and there is no existing `User` with that `user_pk`. Is there any way for this to work? 

Presumably I would need to create a new `User` object on the DRF service the first time the service receives the jwt:
```
user, _ = User.objects.get_or_create(username=user_id, is_active=True)
```

Thoughts?

I getting this error ```orig_iat field is required``` while I try to refresh the token. Checking the code the error comes from the serializer [orig_iat](https://github.com/GetBlimp/django-rest-framework-jwt/blob/5cdc62cb817149ff5fb57b6a19a6980b98d0dba7/rest_framework_jwt/serializers.py#L149).

Decoding the payload I noticed the this field is not in the dictionary. The only fields in the dictionary are username, user_id, email and exp. The JWT_ALLOW_REFRESH is set to True.

Although PyTables was not using this variable directly, it was importing it explicitly, and getting rid of it would create more havoc than strictly necessary.  @robbmcleod do you think this can be back again in numexpr for the next release?  We would really appreciate this.

If you have any ideas – feel free to join the discussion. This is largely based on my own vision of the project and a glimpse look at the open issues and PRs.

The reason I'm writing this up is because I'm still struggling to catch up on the open issues, so having a plan in a single list makes it simpler. Eventually this list should be converted to individual issues.

Any help here is really appreciated!

- [ ] Make the project more contributing-friendly
  - [ ] (WIP in #266) Replace nosetest with pytest.
  - [ ] Split tests to two sets: unit and integration. I suppose that this would simplify local development significantly, but it would affect most of the open PRs, so this won't be soon.
  - [ ] Require linting in the Travis build pipelines (requires reformatting a significant part of the codebase, which I'm not really into right now).
  - [ ] Raise test coverage from 75% to at least 90% and fail pipeline when coverage is lower
  - [ ] Loosen some tests (they sometimes break when geocoder services responses change)
  - [ ] Refine CONTRIBUTING.md with info about adding new geocoders (a checklist https://github.com/geopy/geopy/pull/246#discussion_r174676830, running tests locally, refining docs)
- [ ] (WIP in #144) Include faster/more accurate distance computation routines
- [ ] Deal with https (add SSLContext along with ability to explicitly disable TLS verification and passing own context/CAs) https://github.com/geopy/geopy/issues/124#issuecomment-294558848
- [ ] Try to get 0 skipped tests on Travis. Some API Keys are missing right now.
- [ ] Pull up docstrings. Some parameters are missing, some URLs are obsolete.
- [ ] Be consistent with exceptions (i.e. always raise an appropriate one instead of a generic GeocoderServiceError)
- [ ] Add an ability to rate limit requests in a simple way (and automatically retry on `429 Too Many Requests`). This is especially useful for bulk-geocoding a dataset with pandas
- [ ] Add some utility method for overriding a default user agent globally for all geocoders

### Would be nice to have:
- [ ] A summary for each geocoder class (mostly around Terms of Use).
- [ ] (I'm still reasoning about this one) Add `contrib` package with some integrations. For example, a caching wrapper for Django would be great.
- [ ] Extend the `Location` class with some new props (like bounding box, zip code, map URL)
- [ ] CLI for both geocoding and distance computations
- [ ] Add some examples to docs for usage with pandas (which [seems to be](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/geopy) a popular topic at Stack Overflow)

### Breaking changes (probably for a version 2 or something):
- [ ] Drop Python 2.7 support (don't be afraid, this is a really far-away goal)
- [ ] Clean up constructor and geocode/reverse methods arguments (stick to the same order, require geocoder-specific args to be kwargs ([with an `*` separating kwargs from positional args](https://stackoverflow.com/a/14298976))
- [ ] Show warnings when Terms of Use are violated (like Nominatim is used with a default user agent).

# Zipline trading algorithm

zipline_logging.push_application()

STOCKS = ['AMD', 'CERN', 'COST', 'DELL', 'GPS', 'INTC', 'MMM']


# On-Line Portfolio Moving Average Reversion

# More info can be found in the corresponding paper:
# http://icml.cc/2012/papers/168.pdf
def initialize(algo, eps=1, window_length=5):
    algo.stocks = STOCKS
    algo.sids = [algo.symbol(symbol) for symbol in algo.stocks]
    algo.m = len(algo.stocks)
    algo.price = {}
    algo.b_t = np.ones(algo.m) / algo.m
    algo.last_desired_port = np.ones(algo.m) / algo.m
    algo.eps = eps
    algo.init = True
    algo.days = 0
    algo.window_length = window_length
    algo.add_transform('mavg', 5)

    algo.set_commission(commission.PerShare(cost=0))


def handle_data(algo, data):
    algo.days += 1
    if algo.days < algo.window_length:
        return

    if algo.init:
        rebalance_portfolio(algo, data, algo.b_t)
        algo.init = False
        return

    m = algo.m

    x_tilde = np.zeros(m)
    b = np.zeros(m)

    # find relative moving average price for each asset
    for i, sid in enumerate(algo.sids):
        price = data[sid].price
        # Relative mean deviation
        x_tilde[i] = data[sid].mavg(algo.window_length) / price

    ###########################
    # Inside of OLMAR (algo 2)
    x_bar = x_tilde.mean()

    # market relative deviation
    mark_rel_dev = x_tilde - x_bar

    # Expected return with current portfolio
    exp_return = np.dot(algo.b_t, x_tilde)
    weight = algo.eps - exp_return
    variability = (np.linalg.norm(mark_rel_dev)) ** 2

    # test for divide-by-zero case
    if variability == 0.0:
        step_size = 0
    else:
        step_size = max(0, weight / variability)

    b = algo.b_t + step_size * mark_rel_dev
    b_norm = simplex_projection(b)
    np.testing.assert_almost_equal(b_norm.sum(), 1)

    rebalance_portfolio(algo, data, b_norm)

    # update portfolio
    algo.b_t = b_norm


def rebalance_portfolio(algo, data, desired_port):
    # rebalance portfolio
    desired_amount = np.zeros_like(desired_port)
    current_amount = np.zeros_like(desired_port)
    prices = np.zeros_like(desired_port)

    if algo.init:
        positions_value = algo.portfolio.starting_cash
    else:
        positions_value = algo.portfolio.positions_value + \
            algo.portfolio.cash

    for i, sid in enumerate(algo.sids):
        current_amount[i] = algo.portfolio.positions[sid].amount
        prices[i] = data[sid].price

    desired_amount = np.round(desired_port * positions_value / prices)

    algo.last_desired_port = desired_port
    diff_amount = desired_amount - current_amount

    for i, sid in enumerate(algo.sids):
        algo.order(sid, diff_amount[i])


def simplex_projection(v, b=1):
    """Projection vectors to the simplex domain

    Implemented according to the paper: Efficient projections onto the
    l1-ball for learning in high dimensions, John Duchi, et al. ICML 2008.
    Implementation Time: 2011 June 17 by Bin@libin AT pmail.ntu.edu.sg
    Optimization Problem: min_{w}\| w - v \|_{2}^{2}
    s.t. sum_{i=1}^{m}=z, w_{i}\geq 0

    Input: A vector v \in R^{m}, and a scalar z > 0 (default=1)
    Output: Projection vector w

    :Example:
    >>> proj = simplex_projection([.4 ,.3, -.4, .5])
    >>> print(proj)
    array([ 0.33333333, 0.23333333, 0. , 0.43333333])
    >>> print(proj.sum())
    1.0

    Original matlab implementation: John Duchi (jduchi@cs.berkeley.edu)
    Python-port: Copyright 2013 by Thomas Wiecki (thomas.wiecki@gmail.com).
    """

    v = np.asarray(v)
    p = len(v)

    # Sort v into u in descending order
    v = (v > 0) * v
    u = np.sort(v)[::-1]
    sv = np.cumsum(u)

    rho = np.where(u > (sv - b) / np.arange(1, p + 1))[0][-1]
    theta = np.max([0, (sv[rho] - b) / (rho + 1)])
    w = (v - theta)
    w[w < 0] = 0
    return w

GalSim is currently kind of a pain to install on some systems.  Most python packages can be installed with pip.  It would be nice to switch GalSim to being able to do that, so `pip install galsim` would go install all the appropriate dependencies along with GalSim. 

The main hurdle as far as making this possible is our boost-python dependency, which [can be hard to install](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/wiki/Installation%20FAQ#why-is-scons-unable-to-run-a-program-built-with-boostpython) correctly.  I believe that if we want GalSim to be pip-installable, we need to get rid of our boost-python stuff.  This is a big job.

I'd like to replace all the boost-python wrapping with cffi, which I use in TreeCorr to wrap its C++ code.  It is very straightforward if all the connections between Python and C++ are through C functions.  In Python, cffi makes calling the C function extremely easy.  Then in C++ files we have the C functions call out to the regular C++ functions that use full C++ classes to do whatever they need to do.  This isn't currently how GalSim's middle layer is structured, but I think it would be doable to convert to that.

Here are a list of proposals to effect the conversion (going through the list of files in pysrc):
- Angle.cpp: Make Angle and AngleUnit pure python.  We can enforce all the type checking in the python layer and always pass radians down into C++.  If desired, we could keep a separate Angle class in C++ that the C function could convert back into, but I doubt that will generally be required.
- Bessel.cpp: These are already just functions, so that's easy to switch.
- Bounds.cpp: The essence of this can be converted to a C struct, which cffi can handle.  All the Python-layer functionality can be implemented in pure Python, and this struct can pass down into C with an image.
- CDModel.cpp: This is already just a function.  Easy to wrap.
- CorrelatedNoise.cpp: The only thing here is a deprecated function.  We can just remove it, since I expect the pip-installable version can be considered version 2.0, and all 1.x deprecated functions removed.
- HSM.cpp: HSMParams and ShapeData can be turned into C structs.  FindAdaptiveMom and EstimateShear are already just functions, so easy.
- Image.cpp: This is the first difficult one.  I think we can reorg some of the code so that Images are always constructed in Python, and we use a numpy array for the underlying data.  Then these can be passed down into C++ via `double*` pointers and the shape information.  We may want to keep a C++-layer Image class as well, which the middle layer can reconstitute before passing it on to C++ functions that work on it.
- Integ.cpp: We may need to sever the connection between these.  We can keep int1d in C++, but it's probably simplest to just switch any python integrations to a scipy integrator rather than try to handle the call-backs from C++ back to Python functions.  OTOH, I might feel inspired to try to figure it out...
- Interpolant.cpp: I think in the Python layer, we don't really ever do anything with these other than pass them around, so it would be fine to make them opaque `void *` C objects that Python can pass around and the middle layer can reconstitute as needed.
- Noise.cpp: I think all the Noise classes should be made pure python and have various functions like `applyPoissonNoise(rng, im.array)` that could be done in C++.  I don't think we have a strong need for any Noise classes in the C++ layer.
- Random.cpp: This one may require a bit of thought how best to implement.  Probably could have Python classes that hold a `void*` pointer to the C++ version of the class and implement the various methods as functions in which the C function would `reinterpret_cast` back into the actual C++ object.  It's a little tedious, but it's how I handled the Field classes in TreeCorr, so I think I know what to do.  However, there may be some sticky points to figure out.
- SB*.cpp: I think in most cases, we can switch the various GSObjects from holding a SBProfile object and calling methods of it to directly calling C functions.  e.g. Rather than `SBProfile.xValue(x,y)`, Gaussian would have `def xValue(self, x, y): return lib.Gaussian_xValue(sigma,x,y) * flux`.  In some cases, where it's helpful to have a stored object (e.g. InterpolatedImage), there might need to be an opaque C++ object that gets built at initialization and passed down to these functions.
- SBProfile.cpp: The draw, drawK, and drawShoot functions will need some extra work.  I think we'd want to keep `fillXGrid`, `fillKGrid`, and `shoot` implemented in C++ for each of the different kinds of GSObject, but most of the front end work in the draw functions could probably be moved into Python.  These are complicated functions, so I anticipate running into some obstacles with this.  In fact, this bit here is the main reason I haven't gotten around to trying to do all this yet.
- Table.cpp: The code in this file already looks a lot like what we'll need for cffi.  Most things here are already wrapped via an intermediary function.  So these would look similar in the cffi implementation.

issue_title: ckanapi.dump.create_datapackage() does not produce spec-compliant data resources
issue_body:
From the [data-package spec](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/):

> Resources in a Data Package are declared on the `resources` property, which is an array of Data Resource objects.

And the [data-resource spec](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-resource/) says:

> At a minimum, a Data Resource requires a `name` property and a `data` property. `name` provides a human- and machine-readable identifier for the Data Package. `data` is an array of URIs to data.

I'm not sure what they mean by "an array of URIs to data", but it's clear that [what `ckanapi` calls `path`](https://github.com/ckan/ckanapi/blob/bbeab41/ckanapi/cli/dump.py#L215) should be called `data` to comply with the spec.

Can you please add the license file in the root of the repo? This is suggested to include the package on conda-forge.

Thank you.

issue_title: Do not require install of static libraries?
issue_body:
Currently, catkin lint gives an error if a library is not installed. This is not needed for static libraries that are not exported.

We get this catkin lint  error:

```
advnav: error: target 'advnav' depends on target 'ANPPmsg26' which is not installed
```

But I defined ANPPmsg26 as a STATIC library:

```
add_library(ANPPmsg26         STATIC src/ANPPmsg26.cpp)
```

And I do not export it via `catkin_package()`, so to me this error is invalid.

If you could fix this: great! Alternatively, it would be great to have a kind of `.catkinlint_exceptions` file, I'll open a new issue for that (see #50).

Given a structure:
```
/collection1/subcollection1/
/collection1/subcollection2/data_object.txt
/collection1/subcollection3/
```
Given a typo in the subcollection which adds a zero while trying to access the data object:
 ``session.data_objects.get('/collection1/subcollection20/data_object.txt')`` 
a ``CollectionDoesNotExist`` exception is raised because the parent collection cannot be found before looking for the data_object. This is unintuitive to me because while, I have asked for a data_object, the error reported is indicating a collection problem.

Maybe this is clear to other folks, but it should at least have clear documentation. See: #46 and #21.

For reference and inspiration, it looks like these exceptions have [changed relatively recently in Python](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-3151/), but typically ``FileNotFoundError`` would be used for both cases.

Log all errors to `stderr`, with the following information:

- Date and time.
- Stack trace.
- Input data and requested computation variables.

A dedicated logging package should be used.

Demande de @iDevelopper

> Les catégories « Autres » sont super chargées et mériteraient d’être éclatées en plusieurs autres catégories (par exemple dans la déclaration d’impôt, Réductions et crédits d’impôt, Déficits, etc…). Même choses pour « Autres » pour l’individu.

Contexte: il s'agit du fichier [datatrees.py](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/blob/a3f85b909ad8ff085ecc30a1b8397dc710d31a70/openfisca_france/model/datatrees.py#L161)

@benjello  Qu’en penses-tu ? Je demande pour le principe.

issue_title: Please remove autogenerated files from the source tree
issue_body:
Hello,

The files created by cython and anything else that is generated in an automated fashion please do not redistribute with source tree. This way one spots more easily when something fails and it allows the now freshly generated Debian package to work with the release of yours directly. Otherwise those files would first need to be removed as the upstream files are not allowed to be changed during the build if it is not a formal patch.

Here the files coming to our attention:

        lib/bx/_seqmapping.c
        lib/bx/align/_core.c
        lib/bx/align/_epo.c
        lib/bx/align/sitemask/_cpg.c
        lib/bx/arrays/array_tree.c
        lib/bx/arrays/bed.c
        lib/bx/arrays/wiggle.c
        lib/bx/bbi/bbi_file.c
        lib/bx/bbi/bigbed_file.c
        lib/bx/bbi/bigwig_file.c
        lib/bx/bbi/bpt_file.c
        lib/bx/bbi/cirtree_file.c
        lib/bx/bitset.c
        lib/bx/intervals/cluster.c
        lib/bx/intervals/intersection.c
        lib/bx/intseq/ngramcount.c
        lib/bx/misc/_seekbzip2.c
        lib/bx/misc/bgzf.c
        lib/bx/motif/_pwm.c
        lib/bx/pwm/_position_weight_matrix.c
        lib/bx/seq/_nib.c
        lib/bx/seq/_twobit.c
        lib/bx/bbi/bbi_file.h

Many thanks and regards,

Steffen
AWS Lambda supports python 3.6 now. https://aws.amazon.com/releasenotes/5198208415517126

Api Gateway support is very nice, however it should be possible to deploy AWS Lambda function with chalice that has not been exposed to the user (without API gateway). 

Appreciate any workaround

The `LambdaDeploymentPackager` prints a failure message when trying to build dependencies [here](https://github.com/aws/chalice/blob/master/chalice/deploy/packager.py#L88), but otherwise proceeds with deploying a (likely broken) Lambda function.

This is a bit cumbersome in a deployment toolchain as my deploy step currently succeeds and smoke-tests then fail. Is there a recommended way to have Chalice error out when packaging fails? I'm happy to send a PR for a `--strict` or `--abort-when-packaging-incomplete` packaging mode of some sort :)

See also #928.

issue_title: 'StreamingBody' does not have the buffer interface
issue_body:
So now I'm trying to download an encrypted file, and I figured I'd pipe it through the `StreamDecryptor`. But when I try this, I get back 

`TypeError: 'StreamingBody' does not have the buffer interface`

I suspect this is because in `def prep_stream_data(data):` (L138:internal/utils/\_\_init\_\_.py), my file like object is checked with `isinstance(data, (file, io.IOBase, six.StringIO))`. But my object is a botocore `StreamingBody`, which doesn't inherit from any of these. So my object gets passed to io.BytesIO and dies because it's a stream and not a buffer.

I'm not sure what this check is specifically requiring, it might be nicer to check for specific methods -- I expected anything with `read()` to work here.

issue_title: runtime checking may slow down dynet
No bounds checking in pickneglogsoftmax (Python, CPU)
issue_body:
Hello,

When `pickneglogsoftmax` or `pickneglogsoftmax_batch` is called with an invalid index, rather than throwing an error, it returns a garbage value. To illustrate:

```
>>> import dynet
[dynet] random seed: 2458451077
[dynet] allocating memory: 512MB
[dynet] memory allocation done.
>>> v = dynet.inputVector([1, 2, 3])
>>> v[0].value()
1.0
>>> v[100].value()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "_dynet.pyx", line 1666, in _dynet.Expression.__getitem__
IndexError: Index too large: 100 > 2
>>> dynet.pickneglogsoftmax(v, 0).value()
2.4076058864593506
>>> dynet.pickneglogsoftmax(v, 100).value()
3.4076058864593506
```

    cmake .. -DEIGEN3_INCLUDE_DIR=../../eigen -DPYTHON=`which python`

to this (which works on Linux)

    cmake .. -DEIGEN3_INCLUDE_DIR=../eigen -DPYTHON=`which python`

I suspect that this is because we're typing `cmake ..`, which already includes a `..`, but it's bizarre that the results are different in the two environments. It'd be nice if we could resolve this, but if not we might want to go back to recommending using the absolute path.

issue_title: [QUESTION] What's the feasibility of something like GridFS (from mongo) for entities greater then 1MB?
issue_body:
MongoDB has implemented a feature called [GridFS](http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/core/gridfs/) for all of their drivers. It overcomes the 16MB BSON-document size limit. The Python code is [here](https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver/tree/master/gridfs).

The 1MB limit on entities can be an issue in some applications and a feature like GridFS would be a great way to overcome that. Is such a feature something that could live in this project?

issue_title: Move pipeline URL from '/_ah/pipeline' to something else
issue_body:
Hello,

The Pipeline library will not work on AppEngine using Managed VMs. I opened a support case on the matter and was immediately shutdown and pointed to this document: https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/python/config/appconfig?hl=en#Python_app_yaml_Reserved_URLs

Ironically, on AppEngine this caveat doesn't seem to affect the Pipeline API, however, it does on Managed VM. I don't know if Google will ever allow the Pipeline API to work on Managed VM using the '/_ah/pipeline' path, but for now, I think moving the Pipeline API to default to another path is required.

To get the Pipeline API working on Managed VM, I had to update all my code starting pipelines to pass in the option `base_path` parameter to the `start()` function. If the code is updated to reflect a new default path (e.g. I used `/_pipeline`), then the Pipeline API should work fine in both AppEngine and Managed VMs.

issue_title: UI does not accomodate jobs with many children
issue_body:
We have jobs that can have 500 children, who themselves can have 500 children etc. This leads to a pipeline comprised of up to 100 000 jobs.

In that situation, the UI attempts to load all the objects in the pipeline tree in one go and exceeds the memory available to the App Engine instance.

A better option would be to progressively load the child jobs (and the attached slots, error entities etc) as they are required, starting with the jobs that are direct children of the pipeline. The child jobs would only be loaded when the user tries to expand part of the UI.

For information, the use case for those 100 000 jobs is iterating over all the users in a Google Apps domain. We can iterate by pages of 500 users, and we span a job for each user.

issue_title: Pub/Sub Subscriber does not catch & retry UNAVAILABLE errors
issue_body:
A basic Pub/Sub message consumer stops consuming messages after a retryable error (see stack trace below, but in short `_Rendezvous: <_Rendezvous of RPC that terminated with (StatusCode.UNAVAILABLE, The service was unable to fulfill your request. Please try again. [code=8a75])>`). The app does not crash but the stream never recovers and continue to receive messages. Interesting observations; - If I simply turn off WiFi on my laptop and run the same code, it keeps retrying until the machine is connected to the network and functions as expected. This tells me that this is a reaction to the specific StatusCode - The exception sometimes happens on startup sometimes mid-stream. Expected behavior: - The application code would continue retrying to build the streamingPull connection and eventually recover and receive messages. - This would be handled and surfaced as a warning, rather than a thread-killing exception. This might be the same issue as [2683](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/2683). [This comment](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/2683#issuecomment-337876366), in particular, seems like the solution that I would expect the client library to implement. Answers to standard questions: 1. OS type and version MacOS Sierra 10.12.6 2. Python version and virtual environment information `python --version` Python 2.7.10 (running in virtualenv) 3. google-cloud-python version `pip show google-cloud`, `pip show google-` or `pip freeze`

Name: google-cloud
Version: 0.27.0

_Rendezvous: <_Rendezvous of RPC that terminated with (StatusCode.UNAVAILABLE, The service was unable to fulfill your request. Please try again. [code=8a75])>

import time, datetime, sys

issue_title: JarPublish should have the option to restrict push origins
issue_body:
Today JarPublish allows for restriction of push branches, but not - in git terminology - origin.  This can lead to a publish from any master even if it is not associated with the 'golden' origin.  This bit @ericzundel when publishing zinc from pantsbuild/pants recently.

Ideally, a 'golden' origin could be configured and JarPublish would use that.  Alternatively, it could check 'origin' against a list of allowed values and fail the publish if there is no match.

issue_title: install from source instructions should use pip
issue_body:
in README.md, the install from source instructions should use pip instead of setup_tools. e.g. instead of:

`sudo python setup.py install`

we should have:

`pip install -U` (and optionally mention `-e`)

installing this way will be more consistent with pip install of pypi package.

issue_title: [Bug] SAM/CFN returns Circular Dependency Error due to malformed Pseudo Parameter
issue_body:
Hi,

 While creating an application I noticed that SAM complained about **Circular Dependency** in the following template:

```yaml
    MyParameter:
        Type: "AWS::SSM::Parameter"
        Properties: 
            Name: !Sub EdgeSigningSecretBlah-${AWS::Stack}
            Description: My Parameter
            Type: String
            Value: String

    MyFunction:
        Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
        Properties:
          CodeUri: ../my-function/
          Runtime: nodejs6.10
          Handler: app.handler
          Timeout: 5
```

After a double checking the template I realized that there was a typo in the SSM Parameter Name where``${AWS::Stack}`` should have been ``${AWS::StackName}`` - When corrected the stack was deployed and everything worked as expected.

It seems that this is causing some issues when SAM Transformer explodes the resources and CFN returns this unrelated error that causes confusion - It'd be great to get this fixed or maybe we can create a FAQ.md to take note of occurrences like these.

Happy to provide any more details beyond that if necessary

On GAE plaid-python uses urlfetch, which returns a _URLFetchResult object. This object has no json() method.

Readme should read (for the GAE case): 

```
client = Client(client_id='***', secret='***', access_token='usertoken')
response = client.connect_get()
if response.status_code == 200:
    transactions = json.loads(response.content)
```

issue_title: device_names not complete due to parsing bug in FfmpegFormat.Reader._get_cam_inputname()
issue_body:
### Issue:
I am using imageio version 2.2.0 with ffmpeg v3.2.4 on a Windows 10 laptop with an onboard webcam and a video grabber attached through usb. A direct call to ffmpeg lists both video devices, but `imageio.get_reader('<video1>')` raises an IndexError.

### Origin:
I traced this back to the `device_list` in `FfmpegFormat.Reader._get_cam_inputname()`, which contains only one device instead of two.

### Cause:
This is caused by an assumption in the parsing algorithm.

Line 264 in ffmpeg.py relies on `"` being present at the start of a `line` containing a device name: 
    `if line.startswith('"'): ...`

However, ffmpeg v3.2.4 (on my system) also returns 'Alternative name: "......"' (see file [ffmpeg_device_list.txt](https://github.com/imageio/imageio/files/1321996/ffmpeg_device_list.txt)), which causes `in_video_devices` to be set to `False`. As it is never set `True` again, all subsequent video devices are skipped from the list.

### Proposed solution:
A patch that works for me is the following:
```
...
# Parse the result
device_names = []
in_video_devices = False
for line in infos.splitlines():
    if line.startswith('[dshow'):
        line = line.split(']', 1)[1].strip()
        if in_video_devices and line.startswith('"'):
            device_names.append(line[1:-1])
        elif 'video devices' in line:
            in_video_devices = True
        elif 'audio devices' in line:
            in_video_devices = False
...
```
Although I am not sure this covers all possible cases. This patch relies on another assumption, viz. that the `video devices` section is followed by an `audio devices` section.

issue_title: Reading dicomdir may fail if dcm files are in a subfolder
issue_body:
If provided with a directory,  `_can_read` function of `dicom` plugin tries to open all files in the directory in sequence until it finds first dicom file.

This fails if the directory contains a subdirectory and that subdirectory has name that after sorting comes before first dicom file. 

Code tries to open directory with `open` and raises `IOError: [Errno 21] Is a directory: ...`

Related code:
```python
    def _can_read(self, request):
        # If user URI was a directory, we check whether it has a DICOM file
        if os.path.isdir(request.filename):
            files = os.listdir(request.filename)
            files.sort()  # Make it consistent
            if files:
                with open(os.path.join(request.filename, files[0]), 'rb') as f:
                    first_bytes = read_n_bytes(f, 140)
                return first_bytes[128:132] == b'DICM'
            else:
                return False
        # Check
        return request.firstbytes[128:132] == b'DICM'
```

For example dicom case with the shown file hierarchy always raises error:
```
data
├── 4PQUVRCL
│   ├── 1CEDXV2R
│   │   ├── I1000000
│   │   ├── ...
│   │   ├── I9900000
│   │   └── VERSION
│   └── VERSION
├── DICOMDIR
├── LOCKFILE
└── VERSION
```

issue_title: Filter insert/update values by table fields

issue_body:
I current code base quite a lot of ugly hacks (especially in ``compat.py``) for bridging the differences between python 2 and 3. This prompted me in #333 to suggest adding ``six`` to the dependencies. With the following comment:

> (first of, and besides the main content here, I have added six to the requirements. It is a convenience module used by everyone for easier 2 and 3 compatible imports of things that changed name or nature and is written by one of the core maintainers. I suspect we can replace much or our own code that does this, with this more well tested module, but in here, I just use to get a BytesIO object. If you think this must be handled in a separate PR, then let me know)

To this @lawrenceakka replied the following:

> In my view, however, [python-future](http://python-future.org) might be a neater solution. It is also widely-used, but targets only Python2.6 and greater (which is what we need) so it avoids cruft dealing with earlier python versions.  The best part, for me, is that it enables you to write clean and normal python3 code, which will then just work on earlier versions.  In other words, you can just import a module as usual, without having to remember to import from `six.moves`. It also includes more back ported functionality, so we can use more modern constructs without worrying so much.  Have a look at http://python-future.org/faq.html#what-is-the-relationship-between-future-and-six

Since it turned out not to be a no-brainer (adding six) I have moved the discussion here.
We only support one facility, so the label in the top-nav of 'Manage' should be singular.

Any thoughts?

The way that we use observers on the AllWatcher to watch for expected changes in the model is racey.  In most (all?) cases where we use observers, we request a change via the API and then subsequently register an observer to watch for that change to be realized on the model.  However, depending on load and how quickly the model can be updated, the notification on the AllWatcher could come in before the initial API call response is returned and/or handled via async.

issue_title: Error with --email flag deprecation for `docker login`
issue_body:
In the 17.0.6 docker engine update, the -e and --email flags were deprecated. Sending the `-e` flag now results in a 400 error. The client should attempt to check the docker version or re-try without the email flag if it fails. This is a breaking change in the docker application, and should be fixed as soon as possible.

https://docs.docker.com/engine/deprecated/#-e-and---email-flags-on-docker-login

Originally encountered this issue with

```
$(aws ecr get-login)
```

This calls:

```
docker login -u AWS -p <TOKEN> -e none <ECR_URL>
```

Got the following error since docker-py 1.10.3

```
        File "[]/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/docker/client.py", line 89, in __init__

	    super(SSLAdapter, self).__init__(**kwargs)
	exceptions.TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'num_pools'
```
looks like issue comes with 
https://github.com/docker/docker-py/pull/1208/files#diff-5262437d121a30fc85a81c4b1da57e4bR89

requests's HTTPAdapter API is pool_connections for number of connection of the pool

issue_title: Simple Seq2Seq Computes Incorrect Dev Loss
issue_body: When computing the log loss on the dev set, simple_seq2seq does not condition on gold tokens. This means that the loss reported on the dev set is not the negative log likelihood of the gold sequence.

In the freezeThaw [documentation](http://web.mit.edu/music21/doc/moduleReference/moduleFreezeThaw.html#music21.freezeThaw.JSONFreezeThawBase):

```
>>> sf2 = freezeThaw.StreamFreezer(c) # do not reuse StreamFreezers
>>> data2 = sf2.writeStr(fmt='jsonpickle')
>>> st2 = freezeThaw.StreamThawer()
>>> st2.openStr(data2)
>>> s3 = st.stream
>>> len(s3.parts[0].measure(7).notes) == 6
True
```

it seems the s3 line should read:

`>>> s3 = st2.stream`

issue_title: Tests failing because of jsontableschema-py  error
issue_body:
[_jsontableschema-py_ 0.6.2.](https://github.com/frictionlessdata/jsontableschema-py) is raising `ConstraintNotSupported: Field type 'string' does not support the unique constraint`. For more, see [the report from Travis.](https://travis-ci.org/frictionlessdata/goodtables/jobs/130825589)
@pwalsh, should we provide functionality for the `unique` constraint in Schema processor or just drop it for now and wait until _jsontableschema-py_ supports it?

# Overview

It should be investigated - it looks like if inside an interator has been raised an exception the iterator client can't continue an iteration. So even `try: next(iterator), except` will not help. So may be option to ignore parse errors is needed. The same could be for cast errors on jts level.

# Overview

As mentioned here https://github.com/okfn/goodtables/issues/63 we want to support `ODS` format in our tabular data libraries. [MessyTables](https://github.com/okfn/messytables/blob/master/messytables/ods.py) supports this already, so I guess the main thing now is to port that the Tabulator Parser API.

# Tasks

- [x] pick the best parser available to work with ODS
- [x] write ODS parser for `tabulator` (use Excel, Excelx parsers as an example)
- [x] add parser link to the config to activate it for `Stream` interface
- [x] update readme, docstrings
- [x] write tests

# Links

- https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-ods3

Add `alembic>=0.8.0` to `requirements.txt`

> for the master trunk - it looks like the `a10-neutron-lbaas-db-manage` requires a specific version of alembic looks like > 0.8.0 and as such seems like its targeted towards liberty and newer?
> `>=**`
> would be nice if that requirement was done in requirements.txt or test-requirements.txt 
> As it needs: alembic.runtime.environment import `EnvironmentContext` which was added in 0.8.0 - are you guys expecting most of these deps to be satisfied by neutron?

Even if we were to rely on neutron satisfying dependencies, the use of `EnvironmentContext` in `cli.py` can't rely on Neutron having installed a specific version of `alembic`.

Once we start a CoreOS machine with flannel, an overlay network is created for containers. Each container gets a unique IP address.
Here are some changes and proposals :
1. The app container port shouldn't be bind-mounted
2. Publisher should be able to publish the container IP as well as the port
3. The only relevant JobStates are Up, Destroyed, Crashed, Error
4. Scale logic should move from models.py to each scheduler backend
5. Template the user-data to allow custom flannel interfaces and IPs, like "make discovery-url" does
6. Give deis-registry a unique hostname URL, or run it on every host
7. Create a Kubernetes service for each app and let the router read from etcd endpoint of the service. This should avoid writing a custom publisher for Kubernetes.

issue_title: deis domains:add does not work and other problems "Welcome to nginx!"
issue_body:
I am operating under the assumption this is all one bug, but manifest in several different ways...

The first thing I noticed was that adding a domain to my app does not work.  I visit the domain and I get "Welcome to nginx!"

I should tell you that when I brought the cluster up, I was preparing a replacement for a broken cluster that lost quorum and could not add new apps anymore (both clusters are named "deis") so when the new cluster finished coming up with 5 nodes, I had not finished setting up DNS and `*.deis` was still aimed at the old cluster.  I set one of the new nodes in my /etc/hosts to resolve as `deis`, `deis.deis`, `myapp1.deis`, and `myapp2.deis` for testing purposes, registered a new user, and launched my apps preparing for a zero-downtime live migration.

Nothing here did seem like a show-stopper, and I didn't notice the domains issue until I had already cut over DNS, so I went on using my cluster.

I've now added 3 nodes (6,7,8) and removed 3 nodes (1,2,3) from the cluster, following the instructions to clean up their store daemons and monitors; `ceph -s` healthy looks fine, turning the spare machines off and letting apps move back onto the big iron that was my old cluster, now decommissioned and reborn as new-cluster nodes 6, 7, 8.  That went fine, some apps were running on the removed nodes, they came up fine on the new nodes on their own.

I watched one app that was only running on node 1 come back up on another node and the gateway correctly routes requests for that app to its new home.  Great!

But one of my apps that moved homes is no longer accessible.  I just get a 404 from nginx.  I tried destroying the app and recreating it with the same name, no dice.  "Welcome to nginx!" it says now.

Finally I created the app again with a different name, and at that URL (reporter2.deis) I am getting 503.  

In fact it looks like my cluster is not accepting any new apps now.  They deploy with no errors, no errors in the apps' logs, but 503s all around.

I just noticed that I forgot to update my `*.deis` record, I've fixed it now to only point to active hosts, but it didn't help; is there a wrong time and a right time to configure DNS, or does this have nothing at all to do with my 503s?

The only thing I can think of is, I didn't `mds rm` my metadatas, there is a note about this being undocumented at the bottom of: http://docs.deis.io/en/latest/managing_deis/add_remove_host/ which strongly infers that you don't need to remove them manually and it's fine to just 'down' the host once you get to this point.  I've been through some journals in fleetctl and nothing is jumping out at me, but if there's one I can take a closer look at, let me know.

To make it easier to add additional cloud providers, we could rewrite our provision scripts into one script. This could be written using [libcloud](https://libcloud.apache.org/) and would enable us to only concentrate on Deis logic.

Some additional benefits:
- Prevent us from repeating logic
- Remove requirement for users to install a different CLI and scripting language for each contrib/ platform
- Reduce time to implement a new cloud provider
- Have load balancer support for all providers, not just EC2(!?)

This was suggested by @ianblenke

issue_title: "Welcome to nginx!" page after git push
issue_body:
I just pushed my app and getting a "Welcome to nginx!" page on the app's subdomain. Everything was working fine before. I tried `deis ps:scale web=0 && deis ps:scale web=1` and `deisctl restart router@2` (the router on the machine where the app was scheduled) but it didn't work.

deis v1.6.1 on 3 machine ec2 cluster deployed with contrib script.

I'm looking for docs on the etcd interaction in deis. Really, what I'd like to get is a way to declaratively expose a health check endpoint with a TTL and/or status codes (info, warn, error, fatal) from my app. Basically like Consul's health checks.

Since etcd is being used for service runtime configuration and deployment data (right?) - I suppose I'd have to poll/put a token in there every nth second, to make my service visible? (e.g. containing the actual date the PUT was done, so that the control plane knows when to remove my service from load)?

As a pre-req of the update service client, deisctl requires a means by which to quickly determine if the cluster is in a consistent state.

issue_title: Ceph reports "too many PGs per OSD" in Deis 1.6
issue_body:
In testing Deis 1.6, my cluster reports:

```
     health HEALTH_WARN
            too many PGs per OSD (1536 > max 300)
```

This seems to be a new warning in the Hammer release of Ceph, which we're shipping in Deis 1.6. This got me thinking - perhaps we aren't calculating placement group usage properly, and this is contributing to the various issues we're seeing with Ceph performance (particularly on platform start).

I posted a message to the Ceph mailing list asking for clarification: http://lists.ceph.com/pipermail/ceph-users-ceph.com/2015-May/001222.html

We're using the default 12 pools:
```
root at ca-deis-1:/# ceph osd lspools
0 rbd,1 data,2 metadata,3 .rgw.root,4 .rgw.control,5 .rgw,6 .rgw.gc,7 .users.uid,8 .users,9.rgw.buckets.index,10 .rgw.buckets,11 .rgw.buckets.extra,
```

So, I think the calculation is something like:
12 pools * 128 = 1536
12 pools * 32 = 384
12 pools * 16 = 192

It seems that the placement group documentation is unclear. If we should shoot for 300 PGs per OSD, then it seems like we should use a `pg_num` of 16, since even 32 would result in more than 300 per OSD.

However, if this computation is based on the resources of the host, it'll be harder to choose a default value.

issue_title: "deisctl journal" exits without warning on global units
issue_body:
Somehow we lost the fix from #3119, although you still see an appropriate warning for other commands:
```console
$ deisctl status store-metadata
Unable to get status for global unit deis-store-metadata.service. Check the status on the host using systemctl.

$ deisctl ssh store-metadata
Error: Unable to connect to global unit deis-store-metadata.service.

$ deisctl journal store-metadata
$
```

The Deis CLI for users is great, but it's version is matched to the platform version.

Since the platform is progressing rapidly there are many regular updates (yay!) but the workflow for users is a little cumbersome updating every couple of weeks. We've got some users that every time they use `deis` they need to update their CLI!

There's a library to do some self-updating in Go here: https://github.com/sanbornm/go-selfupdate

This lib requires a couple of things: 
* version API to announce current versions for each supported architecture.
* diff or binaries to download each version.

I think adding the version API to currently logged in deis's controller and using generic diff's and binaries from the common S3 would work.

I need to think more carefully about the initial install and situations where a user logs into controllers of multiple versions.

`deisctl` could also make use of this.
